title: Pickup Lines
alt: That shirt looks good on you, but it would look even better stuffed into the neck of a vodka bottle and flung burning through our office building's window.  Let's fucking do it and never look back.
transcript:
[A bar. Beret Guy is the bartender, holding a can or mug. On the counter are a bottle and two mugs. In front of the counter are four barstools. Megan is to the left of the second barstool and Cueball is to the right, talking to her.]
Cueball: If I could rearrange the alphabet, I'd put your sister and I together.

[Another bar scene, shown from a different angle, with the bartender at left of panel and two barstools to the right. Cueball, standing, is talking to a different woman seated at the bar. Cueball points toward the front window of the bar.]
Cueball: Is your father a thief? Because that's totally my Jetta you parked outside.

[Another bar scene. A bar with one mug on it, and one barstool, are visible. Cueball stands talking to Ponytail.]
Cueball: You must be tired, 'cause you've been running through my mind all night.
Cueball: Screaming.
explanation:
This comic adds strange twists to some classic abysmally cheesy s. Warning, terrible puns ahead:

*"If I could rearrange the alphabet, I'd put U and I together."
:Cueball is making a "your sister" joke. A different variation of this line is featured in 1069: Alphabet, and similar "your sister" jokes are made in 49: Want, 273: Electromagnetic Spectrum, 317: That Lovin' Feelin', and panel 20 of 442: xkcd Loves the Discovery Channel.
*"Is your father a thief? Because he stole all of the stars in the sky and put them in your eyes."(Is Your Dad Pick Up Lines)
:A  is a car, and Cueball is implying that the father is an actual thief.
*"You must be tired, 'cause you've been running through my mind all night."
:He adds the word "Screaming," to make the word "running" be literal instead of figurative.

This is yet another example of Beret Guy's bartender job.

The title text refers to a pickup line like this: "That shirt looks good on you, but would look even better on my bedroom floor." But here the shirt is used as fuse for a  thrown into their office.
